---
title: "0. Compare_sequencing_runs"
author: "Joy O'Brien"
date: "2024-11-12"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Determine the variation in seequencing runs using Permanova
library(vegan)

```

```{r}
# Load in the original and resequenced phyloseq objects
phyloseq_original <- readRDS("~/GitHub/Masters_publication/Masters_publication_R/CHECK_dorm1rarefied_original.rds")
phyloseq_reseq <- readRDS("~/GitHub/Masters_publication/Masters_publication_R/CHECK_dorm1rarefied_reseq.rds")
```

```{r}
# Extract OTU tables
# Extract OTU tables and sample data
otu_table_orig <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phyloseq_original))
otu_table_reseq <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phyloseq_reseq))

# Extract sample data
sample_data_orig <- as.data.frame(sample_data(otu_table_orig))
sample_data_reseq <- as.data.frame(sample_data(otu_table_reseq))

# Add a 'run' column to indicate the sequencing run for each sample
sample_data_orig$run <- "Run1"
sample_data_reseq$run <- "Run2"

```

```{r}
# Combine data
# Combine OTU tables (make sure they have matching taxa)
otu_combined <- rbind(otu_table_orig, otu_table_reseq)

# Combine sample data
sample_data_combined <- rbind(sample_data_orig, sample_data_reseq)

# Check that rownames match between otu_combined and sample_data_combined
all(rownames(otu_combined) == rownames(sample_data_combined))  # Should return TRUE

```
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)

# Assuming your phyloseq objects are physeq1 and physeq2

# Extract OTU tables and convert them to data frames
otu_table_orig <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phyloseq_original))
otu_table_reseq <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phyloseq_reseq))

# Get the union of column names (all taxa from both tables)
all_taxa <- union(colnames(otu_table_orig), colnames(otu_table_reseq))

# Add missing taxa as columns with zero counts
otu_table_orig[setdiff(all_taxa, colnames(otu_table_orig))] <- 0
otu_table_reseq[setdiff(all_taxa, colnames(otu_table_reseq))] <- 0

# Ensure columns are in the same order
otu_table_orig <- otu_table_orig[, all_taxa]
otu_table_reseq <- otu_table_reseq[, all_taxa]

# Combine the OTU tables by sequencing run
otu_combined <- rbind(otu_table_orig, otu_table_reseq)

# Extract sample data and add sequencing run metadata
sample_data_orig <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_original))
sample_data_reseq <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_reseq))

# Add a 'run' column to indicate sequencing run for each sample
sample_data_orig$run <- "Run1"
sample_data_reseq$run <- "Run2"

# Combine the sample data
sample_data_combined <- rbind(sample_data_orig, sample_data_reseq)

# Now, you have the combined OTU table and sample data
# Check if row names of OTU table and sample data match
all(rownames(otu_combined) == rownames(sample_data_combined))

# Calculate the Bray-Curtis dissimilarity matrix
dist_matrix <- vegdist(otu_combined, method = "bray")

# Check if row names in OTU table match sample data
all(rownames(otu_combined) == rownames(sample_data_combined))

# Align row names between OTU table and sample metadata
otu_combined <- otu_combined[rownames(sample_data_combined), ]
dim(otu_combined)
dim(sample_data_combined)

# Perform PERMANOVA to test if sequencing run significantly affects community composition
permanova_result <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ run, data = sample_data_combined, permutations = 999)

# Print the results
print(permanova_result)

```
```{r}
# Extract OTU tables as data frames
otu_table_orig <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phyloseq_original))
otu_table_reseq <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phyloseq_reseq))

# Get the union of column names (all unique taxa from both runs)
all_taxa <- union(colnames(otu_table_orig), colnames(otu_table_reseq))

# Add missing taxa as columns with zero counts
otu_table_orig[setdiff(all_taxa, colnames(otu_table_orig))] <- 0
otu_table_reseq[setdiff(all_taxa, colnames(otu_table_reseq))] <- 0

# Ensure columns are in the same order (union of all taxa)
otu_table_orig <- otu_table_orig[, all_taxa]
otu_table_reseq <- otu_table_reseq[, all_taxa]

# Extract sample data (metadata)
sample_data_orig <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_original))
sample_data_reseq <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_reseq))

# Add a 'run' column to indicate sequencing run for each sample
sample_data_orig$run <- "Run1"
sample_data_reseq$run <- "Run2"

# Combine the sample data
sample_data_combined <- rbind(sample_data_orig, sample_data_reseq)

# Get the common samples between the two runs
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(sample_data_orig), rownames(sample_data_reseq))

# Subset OTU tables and metadata to include only common samples
otu_combined <- rbind(otu_table_orig[common_samples, ], otu_table_reseq[common_samples, ])
sample_data_combined <- sample_data_combined[common_samples, ]

# Replace NA values with zeros in the OTU table
otu_combined[is.na(otu_combined)] <- 0

# Calculate Bray-Curtis dissimilarity matrix
dist_matrix <- vegdist(otu_combined, method = "bray")

# Check if 'run' is a factor and convert it if necessary
sample_data_combined$run <- as.factor(sample_data_combined$run)
# Check the levels of the 'run' factor
levels(sample_data_combined$run)
# Perform PERMANOVA to test if sequencing run affects community composition
permanova_result <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ run, data = sample_data_combined, permutations = 999)

# Print the results
print(permanova_result)

```
